description: Need design for compile-fail on enum field access in render templates (value.<field> on enums). Currently no error; research how to detect/validate in baml-bridge-derive render template validation.
title: Investigate compile-time Jinja enum field access validation

acceptance_criteria:
1. baml-bridge dependency added to Cargo.toml
2. Re-exports in lib.rs compile successfully
3. cargo check passes for dspy-rs crate
description:
# Purpose

Establish the bridge between dspy-rs and baml-bridge crates. This is foundational scaffolding that enables all subsequent work.

## Why This Matters

The derive(Signature) macro will generate code that references types like `TypeIR`, `Constraint`, `OutputFormatContent`. These must be accessible via `dspy_rs::` paths so:
1. User code doesn't need to depend on baml-bridge directly
2. Macro-generated code has stable import paths
3. Version coupling is managed in one place (dspy-rs Cargo.toml)

## Technical Context

baml-bridge exposes:
- `BamlType` derive macro + trait
- `baml_types::{TypeIR, BamlValue, Constraint, ...}`
- `internal_baml_jinja::types::{OutputFormatContent, RenderOptions}`
- `jsonish::deserializer::...` for parsing

## Risk Assessment
- LOW: This is configuration, not complex logic
- Compile-time validation: cargo check will catch missing re-exports

## Search Context (for future self)
- `baml-bridge/crates/bridge/src/lib.rs:1-30` - public exports
- `crates/dspy-rs/Cargo.toml` - dependency section
- `crates/dspy-rs/src/lib.rs` - existing re-export pattern
title: Phase 0: Foundation - Dependencies and Re-exports
